signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7026 — USD outperformance (+0.717%) vs AUD laggard status (-0.985%) creates a 1.701% G10 spread, the widest in the complex. Despite a 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), AUD/USD is testing the 200-day SMA as commodity-linked weakness dominates. COT LF net long +56,800 (76.5% long) represents a crowded bullish extreme vulnerable to liquidation. Options market confirms downside risk with AUD/USD 25d RR at -1.03 (put skew) and an extreme VRP of +18.4% (IV 27.0% vs HV30 8.6%), signaling aggressive hedging against further AUD depreciation.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Options
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.36 — price holds above the critical 160.00 threshold previously associated with BoJ intervention. A 290bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%) sustains the bid despite strong Japanese GDP data. However, the options market is pricing reversal risk with a 25d RR of -1.10 (moderate put skew), diverging from the crowded COT LF net short of -105,136. This positioning extreme suggests a high-velocity unwind if the BoJ signals a hawkish shift at the June 16 meeting.

signals.title: USD/CAD — BoC Policy + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3948 — 150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) supports the pair ahead of Governor Macklem's press conference. Retail sentiment is at an 89% short extreme, fading the move higher, while COT LF net positioning remains short -49,052. This retail-institutional alignment on the short side typically precedes a continuation of the primary trend; retail capitulation risk rises if price clears 1.4000. Watch for BoC stagflation commentary to catalyze the next leg.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Extreme Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5826 — retail sentiment is 88% long (extreme) while COT LF specs are net short -27,797 (15.1% long). This sharp divergence indicates retail is catching a falling knife against institutional momentum. With a -0.83% real policy rate (accommodative) and a 150bp carry deficit vs USD, NZD remains structurally heavy. Retail long capitulation is the primary risk if 0.5800 is breached.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Funding Stress
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7982 — the pair exhibits an extreme VRP of +26.1% (IV 32.8% vs HV30 6.7%), signaling the options market is pricing a massive volatility expansion relative to recent realized moves. DXY vs SPX correlation at z=+1.46σ suggests USD is being bid as a funding/safe-haven asset rather than on growth. 375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0%) remains the structural floor unless VIX clears 25.
